knowledges.content: Current LLMs are stateless, single-turn inference systems that lack persistent state across sessions.
knowledges.label: setting_llm_stateless
knowledges.title: LLM statelessness

knowledges.content: Autonomous agents require persistent memory for multi-step reasoning, tool utilization, and long-horizon task execution.
knowledges.label: setting_agent_memory_demand
knowledges.title: Agent memory requirement

knowledges.content: Current production memory systems (Mem0, Zep, Letta, A-MEM) use hybrid architectures combining dense vector representations with structured knowledge graphs.
knowledges.label: setting_hybrid_architectures
knowledges.title: Hybrid memory architectures

knowledges.content: Production-grade agentic memory must satisfy: high accuracy, low latency, cost efficiency, and reduced operational complexity.
knowledges.label: setting_production_requirements
knowledges.title: Production memory requirements

knowledges.content: The 'Memory Tax' exists: hybrid graph+vector memory architectures impose cumulative increases in compute cost, latency, and system complexity during ingestion and retrieval, primarily due to LLM-mediated entity extraction, explicit graph schema maintenance, and multi-query retrieval pipelines.
knowledges.label: claim_memory_tax_exists
knowledges.title: Memory tax exists

knowledges.content: Hybrid memory systems require ≥2 LLM calls per memory write for entity extraction and graph construction, resulting in approximately 2-3 seconds of ingestion latency per write.
knowledges.label: claim_llm_ingestion_overhead

metadata.prior_justification: Well-documented architectural characteristic of Mem0/Zep/Letta systems; ≥2 LLM calls per write is by design for entity extraction and graph construction

knowledges.title: LLM ingestion overhead

knowledges.content: Hybrid memory systems that use multi-query and recursive retrieval strategies incur multi-second round-trip retrieval latencies, compared to sub-100ms for single-query vector search.
knowledges.label: claim_multiquery_retrieval_latency
knowledges.title: Multi-query retrieval latency

knowledges.content: Hybrid architectures require provisioning and maintaining separate vector and graph database instances, increasing operational complexity and fixed idle costs, unlike vector-only systems that can scale to zero.
knowledges.label: claim_infrastructure_complexity
knowledges.title: Infrastructure complexity of hybrid systems

knowledges.content: Can a vector-only architecture with optimized information-theoretic retrieval and structured memory typing achieve or surpass the performance of complex hybrid graph+vector systems while eliminating the Memory Tax?
knowledges.label: question_main
knowledges.title: Main research question

knowledges.content: For agentic memory retrieval, is higher recall (broader, noisier context) more effective than higher precision (aggressive filtering), given modern LLMs' in-context reasoning capability?
knowledges.label: question_recall_vs_precision
knowledges.title: Recall vs precision question

knowledges.content: Memanto's retrieval is built on Moorcheh's Information Theoretic Search (ITS) engine, which replaces HNSW-based vector databases with three algorithmic innovations: Maximally Informative Binarization (MIB, 32x compression), Efficient Distance Metric (EDM, information-theoretic distance), and Information Theoretic Score (ITS, normalized [0,1] relevance score).
knowledges.label: setting_its_engine
knowledges.title: ITS engine definition

knowledges.content: Memanto implements a schema with 13 predefined semantic categories (fact, preference, decision, commitment, event, instruction, procedure, relationship, belief, goal, context, observation, correction), each assigned distinct retrieval semantics and priority weighting.
knowledges.label: setting_typed_schema
knowledges.title: 13-category typed memory schema

knowledges.content: Memanto's conflict resolution mechanism detects semantic contradictions when new memories are committed and offers three resolution options: supersede (replace), retain (keep old), or annotate (preserve both with flag).
knowledges.label: setting_conflict_resolution
knowledges.title: Conflict resolution mechanism

knowledges.content: Memanto supports three temporal query modalities: as-of (state at timestamp), changed-since (modifications in time range), and current-only (non-superseded memories). Supersession is non-destructive.
knowledges.label: setting_temporal_versioning
knowledges.title: Temporal versioning definition

knowledges.content: Memanto's six design principles: D1 (Queryable not injectable), D2 (Temporally aware with decay), D3 (Confidence and provenance tracking), D4 (Typed and hierarchical), D5 (Contradiction aware), D6 (Zero overhead ingestion).
knowledges.label: setting_design_principles
knowledges.title: Six design principles

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves zero-cost ingestion: memory is immediately available for retrieval upon write, with no indexing delay, no mandatory LLM extraction, and <10ms latency per write.
knowledges.label: claim_zero_ingestion
knowledges.title: Zero-cost ingestion

knowledges.content: ITS engine provides deterministic, exact-match semantic search, eliminating the non-determinism inherent in ANN-based systems where identical queries can yield varied results. This enables reproducible agent behavior.
knowledges.label: claim_deterministic_retrieval
knowledges.title: Deterministic retrieval

knowledges.content: Maximally Informative Binarization (MIB) compresses high-dimensional float embeddings into compact binary representations with 32x compression and no loss of retrieval-relevant signal.
knowledges.label: claim_mib_no_loss
knowledges.title: MIB lossless compression

knowledges.content: The Information Theoretic Score (ITS) on a normalized [0,1] scale enables deterministic, threshold-based retrieval, eliminating the need for index construction and ensuring instant write-to-search availability.
knowledges.label: claim_its_threshold_deterministic

metadata.prior_justification: ITS engine design claim; deterministic by construction but MIB compression claim unverified by third party

knowledges.title: ITS threshold enables deterministic retrieval

knowledges.content: The 13-category typed memory schema enables type-filtered retrieval and provides implicit priority/decay signals, improving retrieval relevance over untyped vector stores.
knowledges.label: claim_typed_schema_enables_filtered
knowledges.title: Typed schema enables filtered retrieval

knowledges.content: Automated conflict resolution prevents 'constraint drift' — the accumulation of contradictions that degrades agent coherence over long-running deployments.
knowledges.label: claim_conflict_resolution_prevents_drift

metadata.prior_justification: Design claim; conflict detection exists but empirical drift prevention not independently measured in paper

knowledges.title: Conflict resolution prevents constraint drift

knowledges.content: Evaluation uses two benchmarks: LONGMEMEVAL (500 questions across 6 categories, dialogues up to 1M tokens) and LOCOMO (multi-modal dialogue with single-hop, multi-hop, open domain, temporal reasoning).
knowledges.label: setting_benchmarks
knowledges.title: Evaluation benchmarks

knowledges.content: Five-stage progressive ablation: Stage 1 (naive baseline, k=10, threshold=0.15, Claude Sonnet 4), Stage 2 (k=40, threshold=0.10), Stage 3 (prompt optimization from Hindsight), Stage 4 (k=100, threshold=0.05), Stage 5 (Gemini 3 inference model).
knowledges.label: setting_ablation_design
knowledges.title: Ablation study design

knowledges.content: Stage 1 (naive baseline): Memanto achieves 56.6% accuracy on LONGMEMEVAL.
knowledges.label: claim_stage1_longmem
knowledges.title: Stage 1 LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.content: Stage 1 (naive baseline): Memanto achieves 76.2% accuracy on LOCOMO.
knowledges.label: claim_stage1_locomo
knowledges.title: Stage 1 LOCOMO

knowledges.content: Stage 2 (recall expansion, k=40, threshold=0.10): Memanto achieves 77.0% on LONGMEMEVAL (+20.4 pp), the largest single improvement.
knowledges.label: claim_stage2_longmem

metadata.prior_justification: Direct experimental result from Table V, +20.4 pp improvement

knowledges.title: Stage 2 LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.content: Stage 2 (recall expansion): Memanto achieves 82.8% on LOCOMO (+6.6 pp).
knowledges.label: claim_stage2_locomo

metadata.prior_justification: Direct experimental result from Table V, +6.6 pp improvement

knowledges.title: Stage 2 LOCOMO

knowledges.content: Stage 3 (prompt optimization): 79.2% on LONGMEMEVAL (+2.2 pp), marginal gain.
knowledges.label: claim_stage3_longmem

metadata.prior_justification: Direct experimental result, +2.2 pp marginal gain

knowledges.title: Stage 3 LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.content: Stage 3 (prompt optimization): 82.9% on LOCOMO (+0.1 pp), negligible gain.
knowledges.label: claim_stage3_locomo

metadata.prior_justification: Direct experimental result, +0.1 pp negligible gain

knowledges.title: Stage 3 LOCOMO

knowledges.content: Stage 4 (maximum recall, k=100, threshold=0.05): 85.0% on LONGMEMEVAL (+5.8 pp).
knowledges.label: claim_stage4_longmem
knowledges.title: Stage 4 LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.content: Stage 4 (maximum recall): 86.3% on LOCOMO (+3.4 pp).
knowledges.label: claim_stage4_locomo
knowledges.title: Stage 4 LOCOMO

knowledges.content: Stage 5 (Gemini 3 inference model): 89.8% on LONGMEMEVAL (+4.8 pp from Stage 4).
knowledges.label: claim_stage5_longmem
knowledges.title: Stage 5 LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.content: Stage 5 (Gemini 3 inference model): 87.1% on LOCOMO (+0.8 pp from Stage 4).
knowledges.label: claim_stage5_locomo
knowledges.title: Stage 5 LOCOMO

knowledges.content: Recall decisively outweighs precision for agentic memory: Stage 2's recall expansion (k=10→40) produced the largest single improvement (+20.4 pp on LONGMEMEVAL), while Stage 3's prompt optimization added only +2.2 pp. LLMs tolerate noisy retrieval context and benefit more from broader candidate sets than from aggressive filtering.
knowledges.label: claim_recall_over_precision
knowledges.title: Recall outweighs precision

knowledges.content: Recall expansion shows a clear inflection point around k=40 (Figure 6), with diminishing returns beyond this threshold.
knowledges.label: claim_k40_inflection
knowledges.title: k=40 inflection point

knowledges.content: Prompt engineering offers limited improvement when the underlying retrieval system fails to surface relevant content. Stage 3 gained only +2.2/+0.1 pp across benchmarks.
knowledges.label: claim_prompt_limited
knowledges.title: Prompt optimization limited

knowledges.content: Modern LLMs are robust in-context reasoners capable of filtering irrelevant information from expanded retrieval contexts, shifting the engineering focus from pre-computed structural reasoning to efficient semantic recall.
knowledges.label: claim_llm_noise_tolerance
knowledges.title: LLM noise tolerance

knowledges.content: Upgrading the inference model from Claude Sonnet 4 to Gemini 3 added +4.8 pp on LONGMEMEVAL, isolating the inference model's contribution from the memory architecture.
knowledges.label: claim_inference_model_matters
knowledges.title: Inference model contribution

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 100.0% accuracy on LONGMEMEVAL 'Single-session Assistant' queries.
knowledges.label: claim_longmem_single_session_asst
knowledges.title: LONGMEMEVAL single-session assistant

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 95.7% accuracy on LONGMEMEVAL 'Single-session User' queries.
knowledges.label: claim_longmem_single_session_user
knowledges.title: LONGMEMEVAL single-session user

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 81.2% accuracy on LONGMEMEVAL 'Multi-session' queries, the lowest category, reflecting the difficulty of synthesizing information across extended interaction histories.
knowledges.label: claim_longmem_multi_session
knowledges.title: LONGMEMEVAL multi-session

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 92.4% on LOCOMO 'Open Domain' queries.
knowledges.label: claim_locomo_open_domain
knowledges.title: LOCOMO open domain

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 85.4% on LOCOMO 'Temporal' queries.
knowledges.label: claim_locomo_temporal
knowledges.title: LOCOMO temporal

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 78.7% on LOCOMO 'Single-Hop' queries.
knowledges.label: claim_locomo_single_hop
knowledges.title: LOCOMO single-hop

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves 70.8% on LOCOMO 'Multi-Hop' queries, the weakest category.
knowledges.label: claim_locomo_multi_hop
knowledges.title: LOCOMO multi-hop

knowledges.content: Memanto (89.8% LONGMEMEVAL, 87.1% LOCOMO) achieves the highest accuracy among all vector-only systems, surpassing Mem0 by 22.9 pp on LONGMEMEVAL and 20.2 pp on LOCOMO.
knowledges.label: claim_vector_only_top
knowledges.title: Vector-only top performer

knowledges.content: Hindsight (hybrid system) achieves slightly higher overall accuracy (91.4% LONGMEMEVAL, 89.6% LOCOMO) but with maximum architectural complexity score 4/4, requiring dynamic multi-query retrieval and structured reflection passes.
knowledges.label: claim_hindsight_slightly_higher

metadata.prior_justification: From Table IX: Hindsight 91.4% vs Memanto 89.8% on LONGMEMEVAL

knowledges.title: Hindsight slightly higher with complexity

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves competitive accuracy with a simpler architecture (vector-only, single-query, zero-ingestion-cost) compared to Hindsight's maximum-complexity hybrid system. The accuracy gap is only 1.6 pp (LONGMEMEVAL) and 2.5 pp (LOCOMO).
knowledges.label: claim_memanto_competitive_simpler
knowledges.title: Memanto competitive with simpler architecture

knowledges.content: Memanto incurs zero LLM invocations and <10ms latency per write. Mem0-Graph and Zep require ≥2 LLM calls per write with 2-3s ingestion latency.
knowledges.label: claim_ingestion_zero_vs_hybrid

metadata.prior_justification: Direct measurement: 0 LLM calls vs ≥2, <10ms vs 2-3s

knowledges.title: Ingestion cost comparison

knowledges.content: Memanto achieves sub-90ms retrieval with a single query. Multi-query and recursive strategies in hybrid systems lead to multi-second round-trip latencies.
knowledges.label: claim_retrieval_latency_comparison
knowledges.title: Retrieval latency comparison

knowledges.content: Memanto costs $0.50/day for 10K memory operations vs. $2.32/day for Mem0-Graph and $1.70/day for Zep. Moorcheh's serverless architecture allows scaling to zero during idle periods.
knowledges.label: claim_cost_comparison

metadata.prior_justification: Cost model estimate, may vary by deployment

knowledges.title: Daily cost comparison

knowledges.content: Memanto scales to zero during idle periods, eliminating fixed infrastructure costs, unlike competing systems with fixed idle costs.
knowledges.label: claim_idle_cost_zero
knowledges.title: Zero idle cost

knowledges.content: Vector-only architecture with optimized retrieval is sufficient for high-fidelity agent memory, making knowledge graph augmentation unnecessary.
knowledges.label: claim_vector_sufficient
knowledges.title: Vector-only sufficient for agent memory

knowledges.content: Knowledge graph augmentation is necessary for high-fidelity agent memory; vector-only approaches are fundamentally limited.
knowledges.label: claim_graph_necessary

metadata.prior_justification: Contradicted by Memanto achieving within 1.6-2.5 pp of Hindsight without graph augmentation. Multi-hop weakness (70.8%) suggests graphs may help for specific reasoning types, but not necessary overall.

knowledges.title: Graph augmentation necessary

knowledges.content: not_both_true(A, B)
knowledges.label: not_both

knowledges.content: Current benchmarks (LONGMEMEVAL, LOCOMO) focus on conversational settings. Evaluation in non-conversational agentic workflows (research, code generation, multi-agent coordination) is missing.
knowledges.label: claim_benchmark_conversation_only
knowledges.title: Benchmark conversation-only limitation

knowledges.content: Existing benchmarks show rapid convergence of competing systems and potential labeling inconsistencies, indicating a need for more targeted and challenging evaluation protocols.
knowledges.label: claim_benchmark_saturation
knowledges.title: Benchmark saturation

knowledges.content: Final results depend on Gemini 3 as the inference model. Stage 5 contributed +4.8 pp on LONGMEMEVAL, suggesting ongoing improvements in foundational models will shift the differentiator towards retrieval quality.
knowledges.label: claim_inference_model_dependence
knowledges.title: Inference model dependence

knowledges.content: Memanto's performance with thousands of concurrent agents remains untested. The Moorcheh engine has been validated at large scales, but the full Memanto platform under high concurrency is future work.
knowledges.label: claim_scale_untested
knowledges.title: Scale untested

knowledges.content: Agent memories are currently isolated by namespaces. Shared memory across agent teams with appropriate access control is not yet implemented.
knowledges.label: claim_multi_agent_isolation
knowledges.title: Multi-agent memory isolation

knowledges.content: Memanto's weakest category is LOCOMO Multi-Hop at 70.8%, suggesting difficulty with complex cross-reference reasoning that may benefit from graph-based structural links.
knowledges.label: claim_multi_hop_weak
knowledges.title: Multi-hop weak category

knowledges.content: D1 (Queryable not injectable) is validated: agents that actively query memory based on relevance outperform passive context injection, consistent with the recall-over-precision finding.
knowledges.label: claim_d1_validated
knowledges.title: D1 validated

knowledges.content: D4 (Typed and hierarchical) is validated: 13-category schema enables type-filtered retrieval that improves relevance over untyped stores.
knowledges.label: claim_d4_validated
knowledges.title: D4 validated

knowledges.content: D5 (Contradiction aware) is validated: automated conflict resolution prevents constraint drift in long-running deployments.
knowledges.label: claim_d5_validated
knowledges.title: D5 validated

knowledges.content: D6 (Zero overhead ingestion) is validated: Memanto achieves <10ms write latency with zero LLM calls, confirming the design principle.
knowledges.label: claim_d6_validated
knowledges.title: D6 validated

knowledges.content: A vector-only architecture with information-theoretic retrieval, typed memory schema, and conflict resolution can achieve competitive accuracy with simpler architecture than hybrid graph+vector systems, while eliminating the Memory Tax. The key principle is that recall outweighs precision for agentic memory: modern LLMs can filter noisy context, so retrieval systems should prioritize broad coverage over aggressive filtering.
knowledges.label: claim_core_thesis
knowledges.title: Core thesis: vector-only + recall > precision

module_titles.motivation: Introduction and Motivation: The Memory Tax Problem
module_titles.s2_architecture: Architecture: ITS Engine, Typed Schema, and Design Principles
module_titles.s4_comparison: Comparative Results and Memory Tax Quantification

metadata.reason: If vector-only is sufficient, then graph augmentation is not necessary (and vice versa). They cannot both be true for the same task domain.

Memanto eliminates LLM-mediated ingestion by using ITS engine's instant write-to-search capability (@claim_memory_tax_exists). The MIB+EDM+ITS pipeline requires no index construction, removing the 2-3s LLM extraction overhead per write (@claim_llm_ingestion_overhead).

ITS's normalized [0,1] score with threshold-based retrieval produces identical results for identical queries, unlike HNSW's approximate nearest neighbor which is non-deterministic (@claim_its_threshold_deterministic).

Stage 2 recall expansion (+20.4/+6.6 pp, @claim_stage2_longmem, @claim_stage2_locomo) dwarfs Stage 3 prompt optimization (+2.2/+0.1 pp, @claim_stage3_longmem, @claim_stage3_locomo). The 10x difference in improvement magnitude demonstrates that providing broader context to the LLM outweighs refining the retrieval filter.
Stage 2 (k=40) gave +20.4 pp while Stage 4 (k=100) gave only +5.8 pp additional (@claim_stage2_longmem, @claim_stage4_longmem). The marginal gain per additional chunk drops sharply after k≈40.

Stage 3 prompt optimization yielded negligible improvements (+2.2/+0.1 pp, @claim_stage3_longmem, @claim_stage3_locomo) compared to recall expansion, indicating that once retrieval surfaces relevant content, prompt refinement has limited leverage.

The fact that recall expansion with noisier contexts consistently improves accuracy (@claim_recall_over_precision) implies the LLM can filter noise. If the LLM were noise-sensitive, broader retrieval would hurt, not help.
Memanto's final scores of 89.8% LONGMEMEVAL and 87.1% LOCOMO (@claim_stage5_longmem, @claim_stage5_locomo) exceed all other vector-only systems, notably Mem0 by 22.9/20.2 pp.

Memanto's vector-only architecture achieves 89.8% vs Hindsight's 91.4% on LONGMEMEVAL (@claim_vector_only_top, @claim_hindsight_slightly_higher). The 1.6 pp gap is small given Memanto's dramatically simpler architecture (single-query, zero ingestion, no graph DB).

The Memory Tax is now quantified: zero LLM calls + <10ms vs ≥2 LLM calls + 2-3s per write (@claim_ingestion_zero_vs_hybrid), sub-90ms vs multi-second retrieval (@claim_retrieval_latency_comparison), $0.50 vs $1.70-2.32/day (@claim_cost_comparison). These concrete overhead differences confirm the tax's existence and magnitude.

Memanto achieves within 1.6-2.5 pp of the best hybrid system (Hindsight) with a vector-only architecture (@claim_memanto_competitive_simpler). If graph augmentation were necessary, the gap should be much larger.

If vector-only is sufficient for competitive accuracy (@claim_vector_sufficient) and recall outweighs precision for agentic memory (@claim_recall_over_precision), then the core thesis follows: vector-only + high-recall retrieval + typed schema is a simpler, cheaper, competitive architecture.

The recall-over-precision finding (@claim_recall_over_precision) directly validates D1: agents benefit from querying a broad memory store rather than receiving a narrow, pre-filtered context injection.
Typed schema enables filtered retrieval, which directly validates D4's principle of typed and hierarchical memory.
Conflict resolution preventing constraint drift directly validates D5's principle of contradiction awareness.
Zero-cost ingestion (<10ms, no LLM calls) directly validates D6's principle of zero overhead ingestion.
